name: Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the field of the Blue Ensign and the sea around the islands
the Union Flag canton and details of the territorial coat of arms
the crosses of the Union Flag and details of the territorial arms

description: A British Blue Ensign with the Union Flag in the upper hoist canton, defaced in the centre of the fly with the shield of arms of Saint Helena, showing a sailing ship passing a rocky coastline beneath a chief bearing the Saint Helena plover (wirebird).
history: Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha is a British Overseas Territory in the South Atlantic, constituted as a single territory in 2009 (renaming the previous 'Saint Helena and Dependencies'). The territory uses the flag of Saint Helena — a Blue Ensign with the territorial shield, adopted on 4 October 1984 — as its overall flag, while Ascension Island and Tristan da Cunha each have their own distinct Blue Ensigns for local use.

features.symbolism: the British Blue Ensign tradition for British Overseas Territories
the Union Flag, denoting the territory's status as a British Overseas Territory

features.role: shield of arms of Saint Helena
features.symbolism: the territorial shield of Saint Helena: a three-masted East Indiaman ship sailing past a rocky cliff (representing the dramatic coastline of the island), with a Saint Helena plover (the 'wirebird', the island's national bird) standing on a green chief above. Recalls Saint Helena's role as a port of call for East India Company shipping.
